rationale: round/target/git-ref grammar are correct and match the plan. But slug/spec/bead_id/mix[].family only get the clean-single-path-element check (reject empty/./..//\/control-bytes) with no whitespace rejection, while these same values are concatenated (not passed as isolated argv tokens) into buildCommand's shell command-line templates. A value like slug="foo --target evil" passes validatePathElement and buildCommand's leading-dash guard (which only checks the string's own start) unchanged, producing `mindspec panel create foo --target evil --spec X --target realTarget` — a smuggled, attacker-controlled second --target flag ahead of the real one. bead_id has the identical exposure. The file's own comment justifies bead_id's extra shell-metachar guard by 'it flows into a built command line' (line ~136) but that reasoning applies identically to slug/spec, which don't get it — an internal inconsistency. Separately, validateMix checks family for path-cleanliness only, not membership in the {claude,codex} enum; an unrecognized family passes validateArgs and is only rejected later inside runSlot (Step 3), after Step 2's `panel create` has already run and mutated state — violating the stated 'any failure aborts before Step 2' invariant for this field. No test exercises the JS validator logic itself (workflow_test.go is static-analysis-only), so neither gap is caught anywhere.
concrete_changes_required: Reject whitespace (and any additional argv-splitting characters) in validatePathElement (or an equivalent shared check applied to slug, spec, bead_id, and mix[].family), so none of these values can smuggle extra flags/positional args into the single command-line string buildCommand assembles.

In validateMix, reject any entry.family not in the closed enum of known reviewer families ("claude", "codex") so an unrecognized family aborts inside validateArgs, before Step 2's `panel create` runs — not later inside Step 3's runSlot dispatch.
findings: buildCommand's leading-dash guard (`value.startsWith("-")`) only checks whether the whole value begins with a dash; it does not stop a value containing an embedded space followed by a dash-flag from injecting additional flags/arguments once concatenated into the command template (e.g. CMD_PANEL_CREATE's `${verb} ${slug} --spec ${specId} --target ${target}` string).

slug and spec are validated only via validatePathElement (empty/./..//\/control-bytes) with no shell-metachar or whitespace check, even though both are concatenated into the same buildCommand-assembled command string as target and bead_id (which do get the extra shell-metachar guard per the code's own stated rationale).

bead_id gets validateShellSafe (backtick/;/|/&/\n/$() in addition to validatePathElement, but still no whitespace rejection, so ` --bead ${beadId}` can still carry an embedded-space flag injection without tripping any current check.

validateMix checks entry.family for path-element cleanliness only, not for membership in the actual {"claude","codex"} set the workflow understands (runSlot's only two branches) — an invalid-but-clean family value is only caught by runSlot's throw in Step 3, after Step 2's `panel create` (a real CLI mutation: panel.json + BRIEF.md) has already executed, contradicting the 'any failure aborts before Step 2' invariant for this field.
